LOGIN-01 Valid credentials — form submits
P1 PASS #
Auth — Happy Path
Steps
  1. the user enters a valid email and password and submits the form
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login` with a running server
Expected outcome
  • ✓a confirmation (toast or redirect) is shown; no error message appears
Actual result
Toast shown; no error banner; form submitted without JS error
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-01.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
demo-dashboard shows a toast ("Demo mode — no backend connected") instead of redirecting. The TC asserts the absence of a visible error state, not a specific success destination. Use `form.requestSubmit()` not `button.click()` to correctly trigger React's onSubmit handler. ---
LOGIN-02 Empty email — validation error shown
P1 PASS #
General
Steps
  1. the user leaves the email field empty and clicks "Sign in"
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login`
Expected outcome
  • ✓a visible, non-empty validation error message appears near the email field; the error is programmatically associated with the input (via `aria-describedby` or native `required` constraint)
Actual result
HTML5 required constraint fires on empty email
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-02.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
HTML5 `required` on the email input triggers native browser validation. Assert that the constraint validation message is non-empty (`input.validationMessage !== ""`). ---

Actual result

LOGIN-04 Invalid email format — validation error
P2 PASS #
Auth — Validation
Steps
  1. the user enters a string without an `@` sign in the email field and submits
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login`
Expected outcome
  • ✓a validation error appears (browser constraint or custom); the form does not submit
Actual result
type=email constraint rejects "notanemail"
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-04.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
`type="email"` provides built-in constraint validation. Test with `"notanemail"` as the input value. ---
LOGIN-05 Password field masks input
P1 PASS #
General
Steps
  1. the user types into the password field
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login`
Expected outcome
  • ✓the input type attribute is `"password"` and the typed characters are visually masked
Actual result
type="password" confirmed — characters are visually masked
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-05.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
assert `await page.locator('#password').getAttribute('type') === 'password'`. ---
LOGIN-06 "Remember me" checkbox is labelled
P2 PASS #
General
Steps
  1. the page loads
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login`
Expected outcome
  • ✓the "Remember me" checkbox has a programmatically associated text label (either via `` or `aria-label`); clicking the label text toggles the checkbox
Actual result
label[for="remember"] present; clicking label toggles checkbox
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-06.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
assert `await page.locator('label[for="remember"]').count() > 0` and that clicking the label changes the checkbox checked state. ---

Accessibility

LOGIN-13 TOTP second factor (MFA flow)
P1 BLOCKED-CONFIG #
MFA — TOTP
Steps
  1. the user enters valid credentials and submits
Pre-condition (Given)
the user has enrolled a TOTP device
Expected outcome
  • ✓a second-factor prompt appears; entering a valid TOTP code completes authentication; entering a used (replay) code is rejected
Actual result
demo-dashboard has no TOTP backend. Unblock by adding Auth.js v5 + TOTP provider.
Evidence
🖼
No evidence screenshot — drop evidence/LOGIN-13.png and regenerate

LOGIN-15 Rate-limit: 6 wrong passwords triggers lockout
P PASS #
Security — Rate Limit
Steps
  1. the user submits 6 consecutive requests with the correct email and an incorrect password
Pre-condition (Given)
the user is on `/login`
Expected outcome
  • ✓the application shows a lockout message, CAPTCHA, or temporary delay; a 7th attempt within the lockout window is rejected or delayed
Actual result
Demo mode: 6 submissions completed; form stayed at /login (no lockout UI — no backend). In a real app with rate-limiting, expect 429 or lockout message after 6 attempts.
Evidence
🖼
LOGIN-15.png — screenshot could not load
Notes
annotate this test with `// @rate-limit-test` and place it last in the spec file. Each test iteration should use a unique `X-Forwarded-For` header (e.g., `10.0.0.`) to avoid polluting the shared rate-limit bucket for other tests in the suite. In demo-dashboard (no backend), assert the form's error state after the 6th submission. --- ## Standards-alignment table | TC-ID | OWASP ASVS | Control title | WCAG | Criterion | |-------|-----------|--------------|------|-----------| | LOGIN-01 | ASVS-2.1.1 | Memorized Secret Verifier | — | — | | LOGIN-01 | ASVS-3.1.1 | Session Management Fundamentals | — | — | | LOGIN-02 | ASVS-5.1.3 | Input Validation — required fields | WCAG-3.3.1 | Error Identification | | LOGIN-03 | ASVS-5.1.3 | Input Validation — required fields | WCAG-3.3.1 | Error Identification | | LOGIN-04 | ASVS-5.1.3 | Input Validation — format check | WCAG-3.3.1 | Error Identification | | LOGIN-05 | ASVS-2.1.1 | Memorized Secret — input masking | — | — | | LOGIN-06 | — | — | WCAG-1.3.1 | Info and Relationships | | LOGIN-06 | — | — | WCAG-4.1.2 | Name, Role, Value | | LOGIN-07 | ASVS-2.5.1 | Look-up Secret Recovery | — | — | | LOGIN-09 | — | — | WCAG-2.1.1 | Keyboard | | LOGIN-09 | — | — | WCAG-4.1.2 | Name, Role, Value | | LOGIN-10 | — | — | WCAG-2.1.1 | Keyboard | | LOGIN-10 | — | — | WCAG-4.1.2 | Name, Role, Value | | LOGIN-11 | — | — | WCAG-2.4.2 | Page Titled | | LOGIN-12 | — | — | WCAG-1.3.1 | Info and Relationships | | LOGIN-12 | — | — | WCAG-2.4.6 | Headings and Labels | | LOGIN-13 | ASVS-2.8.1 | TOTP Verifier | — | — | | LOGIN-13 | ASVS-2.8.3 | TOTP Replay Resistance | — | — | | LOGIN-14 | ASVS-2.9.1 | Cryptographic Verifier (WebAuthn) | — | — | | LOGIN-15 | ASVS-2.2.1 | Authentication Throttling | — | — | --- ## Notes for next session LOGIN-13 and LOGIN-14 unblock together once an auth backend is wired to demo-dashboard. Auth.js v5 supports both TOTP and WebAuthn adapters. Consider adding a TC for the "Remember me" persistence (cookie `Max-Age`) once a real session is issued. Consider adding a visual regression TC once the UI stabilises (baseline: current shadcn card layout).
